fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Choose an authentication method to access the data source

fields.label: User
fields.description: The username of the data source account

fields.label: Password
fields.description: The password of the data source account

fields.label: Add self-signed certificate
fields.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

fields.label: TLS Client Authentication
fields.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

fields.label: ServerName
fields.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

fields.label: Client Certificate
fields.description: The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

fields.label: Client Key
fields.description: The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

ui.placeholder: Begins with --- RSA PRIVATE KEY CERTIFICATE ---

fields.label: Skip TLS certificate validation
fields.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

fields.label: Custom HTTP Headers
fields.description: Additional HTTP headers sent with every request. Header names are stored in jsonData (httpHeaderName<N>); header values are write-only in secureJsonData (httpHeaderValue<N>).

validations.message: Header name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens.

fields.label: Enable custom health check
fields.description: allow you to have custom health check messages

fields.label: Custom message
fields.description: custom health check message. Leave blank for empty message

fields.label: Custom detail json
fields.description: Leave blank to ignore. Format: { "message": "your message", "verboseMessage":"detailed message"}

fields.label: Skip backend
fields.description: allow you to skip backend call for health check

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials. Both fields are used together when root.basicAuth is true.
TLS mutual auth: enabling jsonData.tlsAuth requires serverName + client cert + client key. See @grafana/plugin-ui TLSClientAuth.tsx.
Self-signed / custom CA verification: enabling jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert requires the CA certificate PEM in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.

Custom health check: when jsonData.customHealthCheckEnabled is true, the four jsonData.customHealthCheck.* sub-fields drive the CheckHealth response (backend at pkg/client/handler_checkhealth.go:20-40; frontend override for skipBackend at src/datasource.ts:32-58).

instructions.msg: The Mock datasource is a testing/debug plugin: its query engine is deterministic (frontend and backend scenarios) and its config surface is intentionally simple. Every jsonData field the editor persists is either (a) a plugin-owned setting for the CustomHealthCheck override or (b) a standard HTTP-settings field written by @grafana/plugin-ui's `Auth` (`convertLegacyAuthProps`) — the plugin's backend `LoadSettings` (`pkg/models/settings.go`) only reads (a); the SDK's `HTTPClientOptions(ctx)` (called at `pkg/client/client.go:21`) reads (b).

Authentication follows Grafana's shared HTTP settings model, not a plugin-owned discriminator. The editor exposes three visible methods (default `visibleMethods = [BasicAuth, OAuthForward, NoAuth]` in `@grafana/plugin-ui AuthMethodSettings.tsx:59-64`): 'Basic authentication' sets root.basicAuth=true and provides root.basicAuthUser + secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword; 'Forward OAuth Identity' sets jsonData.oauthPassThru=true; 'No Authentication' leaves both false. Only one of basicAuth / oauthPassThru should be true at a time — `onAuthMethodSelect` (`utils.ts:44-54`) always writes both flags. The Mock plugin itself does nothing auth-specific — the SDK's HTTPClientOptions applies the credentials to the outgoing HTTP client.

Minimal payload recipes. No auth: {"url":"http://example.com","jsonData":{}}. Basic: {"url":"http://example.com","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<user>","jsonData":{},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<password>"}}. Forward OAuth: {"url":"http://example.com","jsonData":{"oauthPassThru":true}}. Custom health check example: {"url":"http://example.com","jsonData":{"customHealthCheckEnabled":true,"customHealthCheck":{"status":2,"message":"upstream down","details":"{\"verboseMessage\":\"connection refused\"}","skipBackend":false}}}. root.url is not enforced by the Mock backend (Mock never dials it) but the editor and ConnectionSettings component both mark it required.

TLS is independent of the auth method. jsonData.tlsAuth=true enables mTLS and requires jsonData.serverName plus secureJsonData.tlsClientCert and secureJsonData.tlsClientKey. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true enables server-cert verification against a custom CA and requires secureJsonData.tlsCACert. Both can be enabled together. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true bypasses verification entirely and should only be used for testing. All TLS fields are consumed by the SDK's settings.HTTPClientOptions(ctx) call in pkg/client/client.go:21.

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.basicAuthPassword == true) instead of secureJsonData. The Mock plugin itself defines no plugin-specific secrets (`src/types/config.types.ts:8` declares `mockSecureConfigKeys = [] as const`); everything under secureJsonData comes from the shared HTTP-settings set that @grafana/plugin-ui's `Auth` writes.

The Custom HealthCheck block overrides the CheckHealth response for testing purposes. When jsonData.customHealthCheckEnabled=true, the backend (`pkg/client/handler_checkhealth.go:25-35`) returns `status = jsonData.customHealthCheck.status` (0=UNKNOWN, 1=OK, 2=ERROR — mapped via backend.HealthStatus), `message = jsonData.customHealthCheck.message` (falling back to 'health check message not specified' when blank), and `jsonDetails = jsonData.customHealthCheck.details` (opaque bytes). If jsonData.customHealthCheck.skipBackend=true, the frontend (`src/datasource.ts:32-58`) short-circuits testDatasource() and never calls the backend — the backend Config still parses `skipBackend` verbatim but does not read it. Details must be JSON if you want the editor's CodeEditor to preview it; the backend passes it through untouched.

Custom HTTP Headers are modeled by the jsonData_httpHeaders array field using an indexedPair storage mapping: each header's name is written to jsonData.httpHeaderName<N> and its value is written write-only to secureJsonData.httpHeaderValue<N> (starting at N=1), exactly as @grafana/plugin-ui's CustomHeaders component (exposed through the Auth component's convertLegacyAuthProps, utils.ts:188-233) and the SDK's HTTPClientOptions expect. This is category (b) shared HTTP-settings, not a plugin-owned setting. The per-header httpHeaderValue<N> secrets are dynamic and are therefore intentionally not listed among the static SecureJsonDataKeys (settings.go).
